channel.title: Analysis: Cache is king: Announcing lower pricing for Cloud CDN
channel.description:
https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/networking/introducing-lower-simpler-pricing-for-cloud-cdn
I have the impression there is some movement in the CDN world as different companies are offering new services (or improving their existing ones) related to cache & CDN.
A few weeks ago was CloudFlare making this important announcement
https://community.frontity.org/t/analysis-introducing-automatic-platform-optimization-starting-with-wordpress/3075/2
And now Google Cloud make it cheaper.
Have you noticed something similar? Have any of you tried configuring Google Cloud as a Cache layer for a Frontity project?
Besides all of this I like the phrase **"Cache is King"** to talk about performance. Maybe we can use it too, as it exposes in a direct way our reasoning regarding "You can have a good performance with a good Cache Strategy no matter the framework used to build your site" (performance Gatsby vs Frontity, for example)

item.title: Analysis: Cache is king: Announcing lower pricing for Cloud CDN
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.source: Analysis: Cache is king: Announcing lower pricing for Cloud CDN

item.title: Analysis: Cache is king: Announcing lower pricing for Cloud CDN
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.source: Analysis: Cache is king: Announcing lower pricing for Cloud CDN

item.title: Analysis: Cache is king: Announcing lower pricing for Cloud CDN
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Yes, I’ve also felt that lately. There are more people claiming that a good CDN caching strategy is as performant as a static site, which is great because a year ago it felt like we were alone in that battle

Read full topic
item.source: Analysis: Cache is king: Announcing lower pricing for Cloud CDN

item.title: Analysis: Cache is king: Announcing lower pricing for Cloud CDN
item.dc:creator: juanma
item.description:
Google Cloud Blog

Introducing, lower, simpler pricing for Cloud CDN | Google Cloud Blog

We’ve simplified and lowered pricing for Cloud CDN by reducing cache fill charges.

I have the impression there is some movement in the CDN world as different companies are offering new services (or improving their existing ones) related to cache & CDN.

A few weeks ago was CloudFlare making this important announcement

Analysis: Introducing Automatic Platform Optimization, starting with WordPress 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Community
Yeah, I think this is great. We should try it out at some point

And now Google Cloud make it cheaper.

Have you noticed something similar? Have any of you tried configuring Google Cloud as a Cache layer for a Frontity project?

Besides all of this I like the phrase “Cache is King” to talk about performance. Maybe we can use it too, as it exposes in a direct way our reasoning regarding “You can have a good performance with a good Cache Strategy no matter the framework used to build your site” (performance Gatsby vs Frontity, for example)

Read full topic
item.source: Analysis: Cache is king: Announcing lower pricing for Cloud CDN
